commit.message:
feat(analytics): cash-flow engine (#107)

* Initial plan

* feat(analytics): cash-flow engine

Implements computeCashFlow and projectCashFlow as requested in issue
#feat(analytics): cash-flow engine.

- computeCashFlow: buckets inflows/outflows per day/week/month,
  splits recurring vs irregular inflows and recurring vs discretionary
  outflows, computes running balance, provides per-category and
  per-account breakdowns, excludes transfers, traceable to source IDs
- projectCashFlow: forward-projects balance from recurring RecurringItems
  plus one-off IrregularIncomeItem events mapped to specific months
- 21 new unit tests covering all acceptance criteria

Co-authored-by: kayodebristol <3579196+kayodebristol@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ensemble): Posaunenchor Bad Nenndorf (gegr. 1913 als Posaunenchor Kreuzriehe)

Fixes #116

Gegründet am 21. Januar 1913 unter dem Namen »Posaunenchor Kreuzriehe« in
Kreuzriehe; heute Posaunenchor der ev. St.-Godehardi-Gemeinde Bad Nenndorf.

- Typ: posaunenchor
- Gründungsjahr: 1913 (historischer Name Kreuzriehe in Beschreibung erwähnt)
- Leitung: Alessa Holzapfel
- Probenort: Haus der Begegnung, Hauptstraße 36, 31542 Bad Nenndorf
- Proben: dienstags 19:00 Uhr
- Website: kirche-bad-nenndorf.de
- Kontakt: E-Mail + Telefon
- Geo: 52.3374, 9.3814

- Foto: (c) Posaunenchor Bad Nenndorf / ev. Kirche St. Godehardi

commit.message:
fix: use router.push instead of redirect() for case creation navigation

redirect() in server action throws a special error that races with
ClientActionForm's success/catch  causing the success toastflow
to fire before navigation, or navigation to fail entirely.

Instead:
- createCaseAction returns { ok: true, caseId } (no redirect)
- finalizeCreateCase only revalidatePath (no redirect)
- CaseCreateForm uses router.push(/cases/caseId) on success

 page transition

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Polish step flow onboarding transitions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: update interactive continuation to re-check current queue first

When a queue drains, agents now ask:
  '队列 X (id=N) 的所有任务已处理完毕。是否需要继续处理当前队列？'

Choices:
  1. 继续处理当前队列（重新检查是否有新的 Pending 任务加入）
  2. 切换到其他队列
  3. 不，已完成，输出最终报告

This handles the case where new tasks are added to the same queue
while the agent is processing. Updated in:
- instructions/AGENTS.md
- instructions/CLAUDE.md
- AGENTS.md (root)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: separate task content editing from status updates

Introduce dedicated PUT /api/tasks/{id} endpoint for content edits
to cleanly separate concerns:

- PATCH /api/tasks/{id}: status-only updates (used by MCP tool_update)
- PUT /api/tasks/{id}: content edits (title/desc/priority, pending only)
  used by Web UI, TUI, CLI — never exposed through MCP

Changes:
- models.go: split UpdateTaskInput (status-only) and EditTaskInput (content)
- storage interface: add EditTask(ctx, id, EditTaskInput)
- sqlite.go: implement separate EditTask; simplify UpdateTask to status-only
- mock_storage.go: implement EditTask
- manager.go: add EditTask with pending-only enforcement; clean up UpdateTask
- handlers.go: add EditTask handler for PUT; remove ErrCannotEditNonPending from UpdateTask
- routes: register PUT /api/tasks/{id}
- apiclient: EditTask uses PUT; remove UpdateTaskStatus (unused)
- TUI: doEditTask calls client.EditTask (PUT)
- CLI test: TestTasksEdit checks PUT method
- CLAUDE.md: update TUI keybindings, CLI usage, API endpoint docs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: task-queue-mcp
repository.description: A MCP Server for task queue

commit.message:
feat: add task editing and remove Start/Finish/Reset from all UIs

- Add ErrCannotEditNonPending sentinel error
- Extend UpdateTaskInput with Title/Description/Priority pointer fields
- Storage layer handles partial field updates via dynamic SQL SET clauses
- Manager enforces edit-only-on-pending rule
- API handler returns 400 for ErrCannotEditNonPending
- Web UI: replace Start/Finish/Reset buttons with Edit button (pending only)
- Web UI: showTaskModal supports edit mode (PATCH) with field pre-fill
- TUI: replace s/f/r key bindings with 'e' (edit pending task)
- TUI: new viewEditTask state with pre-filled form and doEditTask command
- CLI: replace tasks start/finish/reset with tasks edit --title/--desc/--priority
- apiclient: replace StartTask/FinishTask/UpdateTask(status) with EditTask/UpdateTaskStatus
- Tests: replace TestTasksStart/Finish/Reset with TestTasksEdit

* Initial plan

* Upgrade mocha and add overrides to fix npm security vulnerabilities

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/d7dbd434-2d27-4a08-914d-d8410e3a63e4

* Address Dependabot alerts: upgrade setuptools and actions/checkout

Co-authored-by: BYVoid <245270+BYVoid@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/BYVoid/OpenCC/sessions/fe063606-4785-4a11-ba16-6dcbdaaa7c80

* Drop Python 3.8 support: incompatible with setuptools>=78.1.1

setuptools 78.x requires Python >= 3.9, so building for Python 3.8
fails after the setuptools minimum was raised to 78.1.1. Remove 3.8
from the CI test matrix and all release scripts (Linux, macOS, Windows),
and update the twine upload environments from py3.8 to py3.9.

---------

feat: Tablet first-boot TUI setup wizard

Python curses TUI that runs on first boot:
1. WiFi setup (nmcli scan + connect)
2. Chromium snap install
3. Atlas server discovery (mDNS + manual)
4. Satellite agent configuration
5. Auto-switch to kiosk mode

Eliminates need for Chromium in ISO (saves ~500MB).
Sentinel file ~/.atlas-setup-complete skips on subsequent boots.

fix: Live boot — add ignore_uuid to bypass casper UUID mismatch

Casper's initrd has a baked-in UUID that doesn't match the rebuilt
ISO. Adding ignore_uuid to kernel params tells casper to skip UUID
verification and just find any partition with /casper/*.squashfs.

commit.message:
feat(errors): unified error classification system (Phase 1) (#42)

* Initial plan

* feat(errors): add unified error classification system

- Add internal/errors package with 8 error category codes
- Add Error type with code, message, cause, and context fields
- Add helper functions: IsCode, GetCode, IsRetryable, RecoveryHint
- Add convenience constructors for all 8 categories
- Migrate executor: CommandNotFoundError/CommandFailedError now embed SYSTEM classification
- Migrate enricher: EnrichmentError maps config→CONFIG, llm/parse/template→LLM
- Migrate spring/gin/gozero/grpcprotoc extractors: boundary-level error wrapping
- Migrate publisher: PublishError, ConfigError for readme publisher
- Update CLI root.Execute() to display recovery hints and appropriate exit codes
- Add docs/error-handling.md documentation
- All existing error type assertions still work (backward compatible)

Co-authored-by: spencercjh <29922079+spencercjh@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/spencercjh/spec-forge/sessions/63878e45-1197-4aba-87a7-b1a392bc8a18

* chore: add DCO sign-off

Signed-off-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(errors): address review feedback on error classification

- Fix critical: classify timeout/cancellation errors with forgeerrors.SystemError
- Fix important: use errors.As() instead of direct type assertion in CLI
- Add WithContext chaining test
- Add nil error handling tests (GetCode, IsRetryable)
- Add cross-package error chain integration test
- Add cancellation test with SYSTEM classification assertions
- Extend timeout test to verify SYSTEM code, retryability, and hint presence

Co-authored-by: spencercjh <29922079+spencercjh@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/spencercjh/spec-forge/sessions/ddbdd599-04da-4c69-a910-49f5a443c46e

* chore: add DCO sign-off

Signed-off-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docs/error-handling.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update internal/errors/doc.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(publisher,executor): remove duplicate error embedding in classified messages

- publisher/readme.go: messages no longer embed err via %v when err is
  also passed as cause to PublishError, preventing the underlying error
  from appearing twice in the final string
- executor/executor.go: remove redundant else-if (already inside
  if err != nil) and remove err from the no-output message since it is
  carried as cause in the forgeerrors.SystemError
- docs/error-handling.md, internal/errors/doc.go: replace direct type
  assertion with errors.As() in code examples to match intended usage

Co-authored-by: spencercjh <29922079+spencercjh@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/spencercjh/spec-forge/sessions/058acf63-7544-4004-ba0d-4ce22979bc98

* fix(publisher): correct error codes for nil inputs and rdme-not-found; restore grpc-protoc-demo deps

- spec == nil → SystemError (programmer error, not a publish error)
- opts.ReadMe == nil → ConfigError (missing configuration, not a publish error)
- CommandNotFoundError → SystemError (rdme not installed is a system/tooling issue,
  not a remote publish failure; SYSTEM hint says 'ensure required tools are installed')
- Restore accidentally removed require block in grpc-protoc-demo/go.mod

Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

Co-authored-by: spencercjh <29922079+spencercjh@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/spencercjh/spec-forge/sessions/c35aab86-6497-4b7a-b05a-2f76bfda2de8

* docs: remove error-handling.md (covered by internal/errors/doc.go godoc)

Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

Co-authored-by: spencercjh <29922079+spencercjh@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/spencercjh/spec-forge/sessions/355960e4-90db-4756-87c9-3d1f13dbb566

* fix(errors): walk full chain in IsCode; preserve SYSTEM in wrapExecuteError

- IsCode now walks the complete unwrap chain (manual loop) instead of
  stopping at the first *Error found by errors.As, so
  IsCode(detectErr(systemErr), CodeSystem) correctly returns true.
- Add nested-classified-error test for IsCode.
- wrapExecuteError now preserves already-classified errors (e.g. SYSTEM
  timeouts from executor.Execute) instead of re-wrapping them as PUBLISH.

Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>

Co-authored-by: spencercjh <29922079+spencercjh@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/spencercjh/spec-forge/sessions/e3b789f8-3a55-4614-badd-49e1227111fa

* fix(errors): resolve lint issues and improve IsCode chain traversal

- Use errors.AsType instead of type assertion in IsCode for wrapped errors
- Use maps.Copy for context copying in WithContext
- Use errors.Is instead of direct comparison in test
- Replace fmt.Sprintf with string concatenation in publisher
- Remove unused fmt import from readme.go
- Clean up grpc-protoc-demo go.mod dependencies

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* fix(errors): update error handling to use errors.AsType for type assertions

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* docs: introduce two superpowers plans

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* fix(errors): handle nil receiver in WithContext and correct spec validation

- Add nil receiver check in Error.WithContext to prevent panic
- Return SYSTEM error with context when receiver is nil
- Add test case for WithContext nil receiver handling
- Use PublishError for nil spec in publisher (already retryable PUBLISH code)

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* fix(publisher): use modern Go 1.26 errors.AsType with nolint for errcheck false positive

- Replace errors.As with errors.AsType for type-safe error checking
- Add nolint:errcheck comments for Go 1.26's errors.AsType which returns (T, bool) not error
- Change spec nil validation from PublishError to ValidateError (correct classification)
- Extract combineOutput helper to reduce code duplication
- Preserve SYSTEM classification for command failures using fmt.Errorf wrapping

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* docs(errors): update doc.go to use Go 1.26 errors.AsType

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* feat(errors): complete Phase 1 error classification migration

Migrate remaining packages to use unified error classification:

Extractors:
- spring/detector.go: Use forgeerrors.DetectError for all detection errors
- spring/patcher.go: Use forgeerrors.PatchError for all patching errors
- gin/detector.go: Use forgeerrors.DetectError for all detection errors
- gozero/detector.go: Use forgeerrors.DetectError for all detection errors
- gozero/patcher.go: Use forgeerrors.PatchError for goctl check failures
- grpcprotoc/detector.go: Use forgeerrors.DetectError for all detection errors
- grpcprotoc/patcher.go: Use forgeerrors.PatchError for protoc check failures

Tests:
- Add integration-tests/error_classification_test.go for e2e error classification tests
- Add internal/errors/backward_compat_test.go to verify errors.As still works

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* fix(extractor): avoid double error classification in extractor wrappers

Extractor.Detect/Patch/Generate methods were wrapping errors that
already came classified from their sub-components (detector, patcher,
generator). This caused unnecessary double-wrapping.

Now these methods pass through the already-classified errors unchanged.

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* fix(grpcprotoc): preserve error context in patcher tool checks

When protoc or protoc-gen-connect-openapi is not found, pass the
original error as cause instead of nil to preserve the error chain.

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

* test(e2e): fix package naming consistency in error classification test

Use e2e_test package (external test package) to match other test files
in integration-tests directory.

Signed-off-by: spencercjh <spencercjh@gmail.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: LoRA manager — discover and register only, no Ollama composition

LoRA adapters are in HuggingFace safetensors format. Ollama needs
GGUF format. External tooling handles conversion. Atlas just:
1. Discovers adapters on disk (adapter_config.json)
2. Registers them in model_registry DB
3. Shows them in admin UI
4. Links composed Ollama models when they appear

Removed LORA_AUTO_COMPOSE env var — discovery is always on.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

touchscreen (libinput), power management, NetworkManager.

auto-login, screen blank disable, mDNS, install-to-disk.

SSH key generation failures, console blanking problems.

commit.message:
fix: tablet ISO — blank screen, fstab, SSH, console blanking

Root causes of blank screen:
1. Console screen blanking active (Linux default 10min timeout)
2. Bogus fstab entry causing systemd-remount-fs failure
3. SSH host keys not generated in chroot

Fixes:
- Add consoleblank=0 to ALL GRUB entries
- Add disable-blanking.service (setterm + sysfs)
- .bash_profile disables blanking before starting X
- Empty fstab for live-boot (no disk mounts needed)
- ssh-keygen -A in chroot generates host keys
- New 'Debug - no X' GRUB entry for console troubleshooting
- atlas.nox=1 kernel param skips X, shows debug menu

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Refine zsh theme and prompt UX

- redesign prompt to connected two-line layout with cleaner context/dir/vcs separation
- tune Powerlevel10k colors for One Dark Pro-inspired contrast
- expand and normalize LS_COLORS for broad per-extension file coloring
- remove separator hook and legacy ls color conflicts
- disable executable override so extension colors remain visible across folders

commit.message:
fix: chat completions — don't await async generator from run_pipeline()

run_pipeline() is an async generator (yields tokens), not a coroutine.
Removed erroneous 'await' that caused TypeError on /v1/chat/completions.

- Config validation on PATCH
- needs_setup flag for plugins with missing required fields
- 37 new tests

commit.message:
fix: Surface Go blank screen — add Intel GPU drivers + Xorg config

Root cause: minimal cloud image had no GPU drivers. X11 started
but couldn't render without mesa/intel driver.

Fixes:
- Install mesa-utils, libgl1-mesa-dri, xserver-xorg-video-intel,
  intel-media-va-driver (VA-API hardware decode)
- Add /etc/X11/xorg.conf.d/20-intel.conf with TearFree + SNA
- Disable screen blanking at Xorg ServerFlags level
- Add --disable-gpu-compositing --ozone-platform=x11 to Chromium
- Log Xorg errors to /tmp/xorg-startup.log for debugging

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
